nova_1.label: Unprocessed or minimally processed foods
nova_1.description: Single ingredient foods, or foods altered only by removal, drying, freezing, pasteurising, or grinding — no added substances.

nova_2.label: Processed culinary ingredients
nova_2.description: Substances extracted from nova_1 foods or from nature, used to prepare and season food: oils, butter, sugar, salt, vinegar.

nova_3.label: Processed foods
nova_3.description: Nova_1 foods with nova_2 ingredients added (canned vegetables in brine, cheese, fresh bread, cured meats, canned fish) — typically 2-5 recognisable ingredients, no cosmetic/functional additive cocktail.

nova_4.label: Ultra-processed foods (UPF)
nova_4.description: Industrial formulations typically with five or more ingredients, including substances not used in home cooking (hydrogenated oils, modified starches, protein isolates) and/or cosmetic additives (colours, flavour enhancers, emulsifiers, sweeteners, anti-foaming/bulking/carbonating agents) whose function is to make the final product palatable or shelf-stable rather than nutritious.

notes: This is a lightweight heuristic reproduction of Monteiro et al.'s NOVA classification for demo/MVP purposes. The Nebius-hosted fine-tuned model (see training/) is trained on Open Food Facts NOVA-labelled products to generalise beyond these keyword rules.
